sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Home Depot partner files for bankruptcy and closes US stores — leaving customers with thousands of dollars at stake
feed.summary: Wren Kitchens, a Home Depot partner brand, has filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy liquidation and abruptly closed all 15 of its US retail stores, leaving customers potentially out of thousands of dollars and former employees without notice. Home Depot stated they were not informed of the closure in advance and are evaluating how this affects Wren customers. A class-action lawsuit has been filed against Wren Kitchens, alleging a violation of the WARN Act for not providing a 60-day notice for layoffs.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Adjusts Consolidated Edison Price Target to $105 From $106, Maintains Underweight Rating
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has lowered its price target for Consolidated Edison to $105 from $106, while maintaining an "Underweight" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a revised outlook from the financial services firm regarding the utility company's valuation. The report comes from MT Newswires and was published shortly before the article's crawl time.

feed.title: Brown & Brown stock hits 52-week low at 63.28 USD
feed.summary: Brown & Brown Inc. (BRO) stock has reached a 52-week low of $63.28, marking a 42% decline over the past year despite the company's 33 consecutive years of dividend increases. This downturn comes as the insurance brokerage firm announces the appointment of Eileen Akerson as chief legal officer and releases its annual Global Impact Report, with analysts maintaining a "Buy" rating and price target of $100.

feed.title: Acadiana Veteran Alliance Receives $30,000 Donation from Philip Morris International U.S. to Strengthen Veteran Entrepreneurship
feed.summary: The Acadiana Veteran Alliance (AVA) received a $30,000 donation from Philip Morris International's U.S. businesses (PMI U.S.) to boost veteran entrepreneurship. This funding supports AVA's Mission: Launch program, which equips veterans with the skills and networks for business success, coinciding with the program's first cohort graduation. The donation emphasizes PMI U.S.'s commitment to pragmatic philanthropy, aiming for measurable results in supporting the military community, with over $7 million invested since 2022.

feed.title: Boston Scientific stock hits 52-week low at 59.32 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Boston Scientific's stock has hit a new 52-week low of $59.32, marking a 41.89% decrease over the past year, despite InvestingPro data suggesting it may be undervalued with a fair value of $74.84. This decline comes amidst a general downturn in the medical device sector and follows the company's Q1 revenue increase and a $2 billion share repurchase program, though guidance for 2026 organic growth was reduced, leading several analysts to lower price targets.

feed.title: New Ford F-150 3.0L ‘Nano’ V6 Coming for Next-Gen Truck?
feed.summary: With the 2028 Ford F-150 redesign approaching, speculation is rising if Ford will replace the 2.7L V6 with a 3.0L "Nano" V6, currently used in SUVs and Raptor models. This article explores the engine's power, size, and potential advantages like increased towing confidence and marketing appeal, while also considering downsides such as cost and proximity to the existing 3.5L EcoBoost. The author concludes that while the 3.0L offers compelling arguments, Ford's final decision remains to be seen.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Centene Q1 2026 results exceed expectations By Investing.com
feed.summary: Centene Corporation reported strong first-quarter 2026 results, surpassing analyst expectations with an adjusted EPS of $3.37 against a forecast of $2.13 and revenues of $49.94 billion versus an anticipated $47.58 billion. The company raised its full-year 2026 EPS guidance to greater than $3.40, driven by significant contributions from Medicaid expansion in Texas and improved performance across its segments. Centene's stock rose over 6% in pre-market trading following the announcement, reflecting positive investor sentiment regarding the company's strategic initiatives and financial outlook.

feed.title: Hilton (NYSE: HLT) grows Q1 2026 profit, boosts buybacks and EBITDA
feed.summary: Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. (NYSE: HLT) reported strong Q1 2026 results, with revenue increasing to $2.94 billion and net income rising to $383 million. The company's diluted EPS and Adjusted EBITDA both saw significant growth, driven by higher fees and improved hotel performance, particularly a 3.6% increase in system-wide RevPAR. Hilton also enhanced shareholder returns by repurchasing $825 million worth of shares and maintaining robust liquidity with $619 million in cash.

feed.title: News | RTX's Raytheon delivers second missile-warning sensor to U.S. Space Force
feed.summary: Raytheon, an RTX business, has delivered its second missile-warning sensor for the U.S. Space Force's Next-Generation Overhead Persistent Infrared (Next-Gen OPIR) Geosynchronous Earth Orbit (GEO) Block 0 satellite program. These satellites, known as NGG, will enhance missile warning and tracking capabilities against emerging space-based threats, including hypersonic weapon systems. Raytheon's advanced optical designs and algorithms enable improved sensitivity and tracking performance for these critical defense systems.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean launches new credit cards with up to 70,000 point bonus
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has launched two new credit cards, Royal ONE and Royal ONE Plus, to replace its existing offering. These cards provide cruise fans with opportunities to earn points for free cruises, onboard credit, and other benefits across all three Royal Caribbean Group brands: Royal Caribbean International, Celebrity Cruises, and Silversea. The Royal ONE card has no annual fee and offers a 45,000-point bonus, while the Royal ONE Plus card has a $99 annual fee and a 70,000-point bonus, both with spending requirements.

feed.title: Papa Johns Digital Pizza Assistant “Lou AI” Ends Group-Order Gridlock with Smarter, Personalized Picks
feed.summary: Papa Johns has launched Lou AI, a new AI-powered pizza assistant in its app designed to simplify group ordering and personalize customer experiences. Lou AI, built with Google Cloud’s Food Ordering agent, helps users quickly choose items and deals, addressing common challenges like reconciling diverse preferences within a group. This initiative aims to make ordering effortless, allowing friends and families to focus on enjoying time together rather than struggling with order decisions.

feed.title: Franklin Resources stock hits 52-week high at $28.44
feed.summary: Franklin Resources Inc. (NYSE:BEN) stock hit a new 52-week high of $28.44, reflecting a 55.6% total return over the past year, driven by efforts to expand its global footprint and product offerings. Despite a recent decline in assets under management (AUM) due to negative market impacts, the company reported long-term net inflows and announced the acquisition of cryptocurrency investment firm 250 Digital. The stock is considered undervalued with an attractive PEG ratio of 0.39 and offers a 4.79% dividend yield.

feed.title: Above the Noise: FOMO isn’t about ignoring risk
feed.summary: Brian Levitt, Invesco’s Chief Global Market Strategist, argues that FOMO in investing isn't about disregarding risk, but rather understanding the high cost of procrastination while waiting for complete clarity. He suggests that markets are forward-looking and probabilistic, often anticipating future outcomes before official announcements, as evidenced by market reactions to geopolitical events and the private credit market's resilience despite negative headlines. Levitt also addresses concerns about a potential Fed leadership transition and reassures that the U.S. stock market's valuation is only modestly above its 10-year average.

feed.title: Tractor Supply Company stock hits 52-week low at 35.47 USD
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company (TSCO) shares have reached a new 52-week low of $35.47, reflecting a significant decline over the past year. Despite a challenging market and recent analyst downgrades due to weaker-than-expected performance in its Companion Animal segment, InvestingPro analysis indicates the stock may be undervalued. Several firms maintain an Outperform rating, suggesting potential for recovery.

feed.title: Sagtec Global Limited Corrects Earnings Announcement Date for Fiscal Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: Sagtec Global Limited (NASDAQ: SAGT) has issued a correction regarding the announcement date for its fiscal year 2025 financial results, clarifying that the correct date is April 29, 2026, not April 29, 2025, as previously stated. The company's management team will host an earnings call and Q&A webcast on the same day at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time. Sagtec, a technology company specializing in POS systems and enterprise software, emphasized its focus on engaging with investors about its progress and growth.

feed.title: Lululemon adds former Unilever executive Esi Eggleston Bracey to board
feed.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. has appointed former Unilever chief growth and marketing officer Esi Eggleston Bracey to its board amidst ongoing criticism regarding its performance and calls from its founder and an activist investor for leadership changes. Bracey previously held roles at Unilever and sat on the boards of Williams-Sonoma Inc. and Six Flags Entertainment Corp. This appointment follows the recent announcement that former Nike executive Heidi O'Neill will become Lululemon's CEO and a board member in September.

feed.title: Otis Worldwide Corp. stock (US68902V1070): Why does its elevator dominance matter more now for investors?
feed.summary: Otis Worldwide Corp. leads the global elevator and escalator market, offering stable returns and long-term growth for investors due to its essential role in urban development. The company benefits from a dual-revenue model combining new equipment sales with high-margin, long-term service contracts, which provides resilience against economic fluctuations and supports continuous innovation in digital services. Despite potential risks from construction slowdowns and geopolitical tensions, Otis's strong competitive position, global reach, and focus on sustainable and smart technology make it an attractive defensive growth play for diversified portfolios.

feed.title: Cadence Design Systems Stock Q1 2026 Earnings: Every Segment Grew Double Digits
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) reported strong Q1 2026 earnings with a 19% year-over-year revenue increase to $1.474 billion and a record $8 billion backlog. All three business segments—Core EDA, IP, and System Design and Analysis—achieved double-digit growth, driven by AI and HPC demand. The company raised its full-year 2026 guidance for both revenue and non-GAAP EPS, while its TIKR model price target suggests an implied upside of 101%.

feed.title: Utility DTE Energy’s plan to pause Michigan rate increases hinges on Oracle data centre
feed.summary: DTE Energy announced its intention to forego asking for rate increases for at least two years, conditional on the successful operation of a US$16 billion data center campus in Saline Township, Michigan, built for Oracle. This data center, to be powered by DTE Energy and augmented by a battery energy storage system (BESS), is expected to contribute US$9 billion to DTE's electric system improvements, reducing the financial burden on other customers. DTE also detailed a US$474.3 million request to support grid investments, including the conversion of a coal plant to natural gas and the development of the 220MW/880MWh Trenton Channel Energy Center BESS.

feed.title: UBS reiterates Public Storage stock rating on solid Q1 results By Investing.com
feed.summary: UBS has reiterated its Neutral rating and $276.00 price target for Public Storage (NYSE:PSA) following the company's solid first-quarter 2026 results. Despite strong Q1 performance, which included increased average occupancy, the company maintained its full-year outlook due to macroeconomic uncertainties and NSA integration. Public Storage's stock is currently trading at a premium to the subsector and appears overvalued, according to InvestingPro analysis, despite recent earnings surpassing analyst expectations.

feed.title: Roth/MKM raises Cincinnati Financial stock price target on earnings By Investing.com
feed.summary: Roth/MKM has increased its price target for Cincinnati Financial (NASDAQ:CINF) to $190 from $175, maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment follows the insurance company's Q1 operating income of $2.10 per share, which surpassed the consensus estimate of $1.95. The stock is currently trading at $167.13, with InvestingPro analysis suggesting it is undervalued despite higher-than-modeled catastrophe losses.

feed.title: A Look At MGM Resorts (MGM) Valuation As Recent Share Price Momentum Sparks Debate
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International (MGM) shares are experiencing recent momentum, trading around $40.55. While a popular valuation narrative suggests the stock is 45% overvalued with a fair value of $27.97, an alternative discounted cash flow (DCF) model indicates it may be undervalued by 37% at $64.34. This disparity highlights mixed signals on MGM's true valuation, urging investors to evaluate the underlying assumptions.

feed.title: Franklin Resources (NYSE:BEN) Reaches New 12-Month High After Better-Than-Expected Earnings
feed.summary: Franklin Resources (NYSE:BEN) achieved a new 52-week high following better-than-expected Q1 earnings, with EPS of $0.71 against a $0.57 consensus and revenue of $1.75 billion, up 8.7% year-over-year. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.33, representing a 4.7% yield, though its payout ratio is elevated at 122.22%. Analyst coverage for BEN is mixed, with a "Reduce" consensus rating and an average price target below the current stock price.

feed.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities (NYSE:ARE) Sets New 52-Week Low Following Analyst Downgrade
feed.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities (NYSE:ARE) hit a new 52-week low after BNP Paribas Exane downgraded its price target to $44.00. The company's Q1 FFO per share met estimates, but revenue missed, and negative sentiment surrounds tenant wind-downs and ongoing valuation concerns. Despite mixed analyst ratings, the stock yields about 7.2%, and institutions hold a significant portion of shares.

feed.title: Arista Networks (ANET) Expected to Announce Earnings on Tuesday
feed.summary: Arista Networks (ANET) is scheduled to announce its Q1 2026 earnings after market close on Tuesday, May 5th, with analysts expecting $0.81 EPS and $2.6154 billion in revenue. The company previously beat estimates in its last quarter, reporting $0.82 EPS against a $0.75 consensus and $2.49 billion in revenue. The article also notes recent insider selling and hedge fund activity, alongside analyst ratings that largely recommend a "Buy" for ANET.

feed.title: Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY) to Release Quarterly Earnings on Tuesday
feed.summary: Jack Henry & Associates (NASDAQ:JKHY) is expected to announce its Q3 2026 earnings after market close on Tuesday, May 5th, with analysts predicting $1.44 EPS and $616.9120 million in revenue. The company previously reported $1.72 EPS in Q4 2025, beating consensus estimates, and recently increased its quarterly dividend to $0.61 per share. Several analysts have issued "buy" or "outperform" ratings for JKHY shares, with a consensus price target of $200.15.

feed.title: Synopsys Inc. stock (US8716071076): Why AI chip design leadership matters more now
feed.summary: Synopsys Inc. is positioned as a key player in the AI revolution due to its leadership in electronic design automation (EDA) software and semiconductor IP, which are essential for advanced chip design. The company benefits from the surge in AI investments, providing tools that optimize chip development for major tech companies. Synopsys' strong market position, high barriers to entry, and recurring revenue model make it an attractive long-term investment, despite potential risks related to semiconductor cyclicality and geopolitical tensions.

feed.title: Wrong year corrected: Sagtec sets FY2025 results for April 29, 2026
feed.summary: Sagtec Global (NASDAQ: SAGT) corrected its announcement regarding the release of its fiscal year 2025 financial results. The results will now be released on April 29, 2026, instead of the previously misstated April 29, 2025. Management will host an earnings call and Q&A webcast on the corrected date at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time.

feed.title: As Las Vegas Strip goes quiet, neighbourhood casinos stay busy
feed.summary: The Las Vegas Strip is experiencing a significant slowdown in business due to rising costs, subdued consumer sentiment, and international pressures, leading to declining profits for major operators like MGM and Caesars. In contrast, neighborhood casinos, such as Red Rock and Boyd Gaming, are thriving, benefiting from an influx of new residents and increased local spending. Strip operators are responding with price cuts, while analysts suggest that attracting budget-conscious travelers may require more incentives, potentially impacting margins.

feed.title: Gilead Sciences Completes $7.8 Billion Acquisition of Arcellx, Securing Full Control of CAR T Therapy Anito-cel
feed.summary: Gilead Sciences has completed its $7.8 billion acquisition of Arcellx, gaining full control of the investigational CAR T-cell therapy anitocabtagene autoleucel (anito-cel) for multiple myeloma. This strategic move aims to streamline the development and commercialization of anito-cel through Gilead's Kite oncology unit, eliminating future profit-sharing obligations. The deal is expected to be dilutive to Gilead's earnings per share in the short term but accretive by 2028, reflecting its broader oncology strategy and expansion in cell therapy.

feed.title: Kimmeridge says 'time for action' at Devon Energy, urges asset sales after Coterra merger (DVN:NYSE)
feed.summary: Investment firm Kimmeridge has issued an open letter to the incoming board of Devon Energy, calling for immediate action to sell non-core assets and improve capital allocation following its merger with Coterra. Kimmeridge's recommendations are aimed at enhancing value for Devon Energy shareholders.

feed.title: UBS raises Packaging Corp. of America price target on execution By Investing.com
feed.summary: UBS raised its price target for Packaging Corp. of America (NYSE:PKG) to $232 from $225, maintaining a Neutral rating, citing the company's strong execution, market share gains, and over 20% EBITDA margins. Despite this optimism, the firm notes valuation concerns, with PKG trading at a high P/E ratio of 25.97 and a Price/Book multiple of 4.09, suggesting it is overvalued according to InvestingPro data. Other recent analyst actions include Truist Securities lowering its price target due to second-quarter guidance falling short of estimates, while BofA Securities increased its target, praising the company's volume growth.

feed.title: FedEx prepares to reactivate grounded MD-11 fleet in May
feed.summary: FedEx is preparing to reactivate its grounded fleet of 28 MD-11 freighter aircraft by the end of May, working with Boeing to install a new bearing to correct a pylon design flaw. This move comes despite regulators not yet lifting a mandatory grounding initiated after a fatal UPS MD-11 crash in November. FedEx plans to discuss the repair and safety actions with flight crews and remains optimistic about the aircraft's return to service, contrasting with UPS's decision to scrap its MD-11 fleet.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive’s 2030 Strategy: A Healthier Future
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive has unveiled its 2030 Sustainability & Social Impact Strategy, building on its prior achievements like the recyclable toothpaste tube and its Bright Smiles, Bright Futures program. The new five-year roadmap focuses on driving social impact, improving millions of homes, and preserving the environment, with key areas including climate resilience, plastic waste elimination, water security, and promoting hygiene and oral health habits. Chief Sustainability Officer Ann Tracy emphasizes the strategy's role in fostering growth for the business and communities by taking a science-based, pragmatic, and value-driven approach.

feed.title: Steel Dynamics Inc. stock (US8581191009): Why does its steel recycling edge matter more now for U.S.
feed.summary: Steel Dynamics Inc. is well-positioned for growth in the U.S. steel sector due to its recycling-focused business model utilizing electric arc furnaces, which offers cost efficiencies and sustainability advantages. The company's strategy aligns with rising demand for green steel and U.S. infrastructure spending, providing investors exposure to domestic manufacturing and sustainable trends. Analysts generally view Steel Dynamics favorably, highlighting its resilient model and disciplined capital allocation.

feed.title: Sherwin-Williams Releases Q1 2026 Financial Results
feed.summary: The Sherwin-Williams Company announced Q1 2026 financial results, exceeding Wall Street expectations with adjusted earnings of $2.35 per share on revenues of $5.67 billion. The Paint Stores Group segment was a key driver, seeing a 3.7% year-over-year increase in revenue and 2.4% same-store sales growth. The company provided full-year 2026 adjusted EPS guidance between $11.50 and $11.90, reinforcing confidence in sustained demand.

feed.title: AbbVie Submits sNDA for Upadacitinib in Adults and Adolescents With Severe Alopecia Areata
feed.summary: AbbVie has submitted a supplemental new drug application (sNDA) to the FDA for upadacitinib (Rinvoq) to treat severe alopecia areata in adults and adolescents. This submission is based on phase 3 UP-AA trial data, which demonstrated significant scalp hair regrowth, including complete coverage in many patients. If approved, upadacitinib would be the second JAK inhibitor indicated for alopecia areata in the US, with trial results suggesting a new benchmark for efficacy.

feed.title: Bill Ford says Trump’s push to renew U.S. manufacturing will boost supplier base
feed.summary: Bill Ford, Executive Chair of Ford Motor Co., believes that former President Trump's efforts to revitalize U.S. manufacturing will positively impact the automotive supplier base. Ford and his daughter Alexandra Ford English spoke at an Economic Club of Grand Rapids event, highlighting how this focus could strengthen domestic production. This initiative is expected to benefit companies supplying parts and services to manufacturers within the United States.

feed.title: Barry Diller’s IAC To Rebrand As People Incorporated, Focus On Publishing Amid Restructuring, Layoffs
feed.summary: Barry Diller's IAC is rebranding as "People Incorporated" and shifting its primary focus to its People Inc. publishing business, which includes iconic brands like People, Food + Wine, and Travel & Leisure. The restructuring involves significantly reducing overhead, with an anticipated $40 million in annual cost savings, and will see People CEO Neil Vogel become the chief executive of the revamped company, with Diller remaining chairman. This move emphasizes digital publishing expertise applied to traditional media assets and is expected to be completed by Q1 2027.

feed.title: Oppenheimer Adjusts Relay Therapeutics Price Target to $18 From $14, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has increased its price target for Relay Therapeutics (RLAY) to $18 from $14, while reiterating an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects continued positive sentiment from analysts towards the biotechnology company. The news was reported by MT Newswires.

feed.title: Southern Company stock (US8425871071): Is clean energy execution now the real test for steady return
feed.summary: Southern Company, a major U.S. utility, is pivoting to clean energy with significant investments in nuclear power and renewables, while maintaining a regulated business model for stable returns. The article examines how these strategies, coupled with surging demand from AI data centers and electrification trends in the high-growth U.S. Southeast, position the company for reliable dividends and long-term investor appeal. It also discusses challenges such as regulatory risks, interest rate sensitivity, and environmental factors.

feed.title: BNP Paribas Exane Has Lowered Expectations for Alexandria Real Estate Equities (NYSE:ARE) Stock Price
feed.summary: BNP Paribas Exane has lowered its price target for Alexandria Real Estate Equities (NYSE:ARE) to $44, maintaining an "underperform" rating, after the company's Q1 results showed FFO/EPS met expectations but revenue missed and declined year-over-year. The stock traded down approximately 8.1% to $41.86, amidst ongoing valuation concerns and negative net margin and return on equity. Despite significant institutional ownership, analysts have a "Hold" consensus rating with an average target of $63.27, while insider transactions show both selling and buying activity.

feed.title: Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical (RARE) Projected to Post Quarterly Earnings on Tuesday
feed.summary: Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical (NASDAQ: RARE) is anticipated to announce its Q1 2026 earnings on Tuesday, May 5th, with analysts forecasting an EPS of ($1.4327) and revenue of $163.96 million. The company's previous quarter saw a revenue increase of 25.5% year-over-year to $207.28 million, despite missing EPS estimates. Institutional investors hold a significant 97.67% of the company's stock, even as insiders have reduced their holdings in the last quarter.

feed.title: EOG Resources (NYSE:EOG) Price Target Raised to $155.00 at Morgan Stanley
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has increased its price target for EOG Resources (NYSE:EOG) from $128.00 to $155.00, reiterating an "equal weight" rating, suggesting a potential 14.55% upside. Other firms like Jefferies and Sanford Bernstein also raised their targets. EOG Resources recently surpassed Q4 earnings and revenue expectations, and the company has an average "Hold" rating from analysts with a consensus target price of $153.68.

feed.title: RLAY Maintained by Oppenheimer -- Price Target Raised to $18.00
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has maintained its "Outperform" rating on Relay Therapeutics (RLAY) and increased its price target to $18.00 from $14.00, signaling confidence in the company's growth potential. However, GuruFocus identifies RLAY as 203.2% overvalued with a GF Value™ of $4.93 compared to its current price of $14.95, and its GF Score™ of 54/100 points to moderate overall performance, held back by low profitability. Insider selling of $0.8 million also suggests caution for investors.

feed.title: McDonald's is the latest fast food chain to offer fancier drinks
feed.summary: McDonald's is expanding its beverage menu in the U.S. with six new "crafted" drinks, including refreshers with boba and crafted sodas with cold foam, to attract more customers and boost afternoon sales. This move follows similar efforts by other fast-food chains like KFC, Wendy's, and Taco Bell, which are also investing in premium drink offerings to compete with coffee shops and capitalize on the profitable beverage market. McDonald's previously tested a dedicated beverage concept called CosMc's, and while those stores closed, the company is now integrating some of those ideas into its main restaurants.

feed.title: Food distributor Sysco misses quarterly sales estimates as dining-out demand cools
feed.summary: Food distributor Sysco reported third-quarter sales of $20.52 billion, falling short of analysts' estimates of $20.57 billion, due to cooling demand from restaurants for packaged foods. Despite maintaining its annual profit forecast, the company's shares dropped as higher costs and incentive compensation pressured margins. Sysco reaffirmed its full-year adjusted earnings per share forecast and recently announced a $29 billion deal to acquire Jetro Restaurant Depot to expand its reach among independent restaurants.

feed.title: Rambus Plunges 15.2% Amid Sector-Wide Selling
feed.summary: Rambus Inc. shares fell 15.2% on Tuesday, closing at $119.58, as part of a broad selloff across semiconductor sector peers. The decline, marked by elevated trading volume, reflects a sector-wide repricing rather than company-specific concerns, though Rambus experienced the steepest drop among its peers. Investors are advised to monitor the sector for stabilization and potential buying opportunities if the selloff becomes overdone relative to fundamentals.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark warns of up to $170 million cost hit from oil prices
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark Corp. anticipates an additional $150 million to $170 million in input expenses if oil prices remain around $100 per barrel throughout the second half of the year. This warning comes despite the company projecting double-digit earnings growth, and these potential costs have not yet been incorporated into their outlook. Kimberly-Clark is the second major household goods manufacturer, following Procter & Gamble Co., to alert about business impacts stemming from higher oil prices, and it plans to offset some of these increases.

feed.title: RadNet, Inc. Announces Date of its First Quarter 2026 Financial Results Conference Call
feed.summary: RadNet, Inc. (NASDAQ: RDNT) announced it will host a conference call on Monday, May 11, 2026, at 7:30 a.m. Pacific Time to discuss its first quarter 2026 financial results. Investors can listen by dialing specified numbers or via webcast. The company is a national leader in diagnostic imaging services and digital health solutions.
